title: RA.999 1morning & Regal86
tracklist:
Dj T-1000 - Supernatural (Bangin')
Mhonolink - Prelude (Original Mix)
The Mathematic Assassins - Snakes And Politics
Surgeon - Part 3
Regal86 - Essential (Unr)
Joey Beltram - Arena
G Flame & Mr G - Just Another B Line
Ben Sims - The Key
Paul Mac - Struggling Event
Percy X - As Is (Original Mix)
1OO1O - Mushroom Samba
Regal86 - Stereorealist
Deetron, Box Blaze - Pocket Rocker (Original Mix)
DJ SPECIAL NEEDS - Lil Louis French Kiss Edit
Thomas Bangalter - What To Do
Jack Mackerel - Chum
Dave Clarke - Before I was So Rudely Interrupted
Mark EG & Chrissi - Converter
Wilko - Bitches
Stanny Franssen - Data String
Hardcell - Echoism
Hardcell - Greet the Sun
Roski - ACKSTA
SLS - Reached (Andreas Saag Mix)

translation.content: When it comes to purveyors of contemporary hardgroove, it's hard to top 1morning and Regal86. The duo have emerged from a buzzy and decidedly funk-oriented techno scene on the American West Coast, repping Los Angeles and Monterrey respectively. Bound by a shared love of old-school flair and intuition behind the decks, you'll often find Regal86 ditching headphones altogether in favour of studying waveforms in real time, while 1morning's vinyl-only sets are steered by the movements of the dance floor and the fire in his heart. So it comes as no surprise that we had to invite them back in session. In the last week before we celebrate the 1000th RA Podcast, we'll drop five back-to-backs over the next few days. From NYC Downlow to Berlin's Bar25, what unifies this cohort is a sense that they capture where dance music has been—and crucially, where it's going. Kicking off the week, we have this exclusive recording from 1morning and Regal86's co-headline show at New York's Bossa Nova Civic Club—the "extra special" final stop of their recent US tour. As the first instalment of RA.999, this one-hour set makes a strong case for what it means to achieve ultimate freedom in the booth. The duo might be known for their raw, swung techno explorations, but here, their more sensual leanings carry the most impact. You'll find hardgroove's very own daddy Ben Sims, Paul Mac's 2002 melodic beauty "Struggling Event" and the lavish stomp of Percy X's "As Is." And, of course, it wouldn't be a Regal86 and 1morning link-up without a healthy dose of Mexico City rising star—and the former's frequent sparring partner—1OO1O. But really, this mix proves just how perceptive the two DJs are. It opens a portal into the beating heart of groove-first techno, masterfully flowing between impulse and restraint, tradition and modernity. And who are we to resist? - Rachel Grace Almeida Can you talk us through how and where this was recorded? Regal86: This was recorded at [New York's] Bossa Nova Civic Club for the sixth volume of my party series, Mystical Blends. It felt extra special since it was the final gig of our co-headlining US tour. 1morning: [Deciding to record this set] was pretty spontaneous but it made sense. How do you feel this session represents your styles, both as a pair and individuals? 1morning: I think it encompasses our love for melody, drama and rhythm in techno. Nowadays, it's so easy for a mix to be full of the same loopy tools. I leaned into a more jacking approach to compliment Regal's percussive ethos. Regal86: I think our styles are similar, but each of us brought something different. This mix shows off a bit of us both. But more than the track selection, what I really enjoy about it is how we each interpret the action and reaction of the crowd. What's your favourite moment in this mix and why? 1morning: I'd have to pick out the part of the mix where I throw in my favourite DJ Rush track. Regal86: I like the energy between the Joey Beltram, G Flame and Mr. G tracks. The bassline is wild and it feels like the tracks are having a conversation. What's a club, party or musical movement that's had a major impact on you as an artist? Regal86: There are a couple of clubs I hold close, but so far, Yu Yu in Mexico City really stands out. I've spent hours playing in this sweaty, underground basement with a solid sound system and an intimate crowd that just didn't want to stop. 1morning: I've been visiting Detroit for the past two and a half years as both a DJ and lover of house music. It's taught me so much about myself as an artist. I tend to just show up to parties I hear are "good" without really viewing the lineup before because I crave a good surprise. I follow my intuition and very rarely am disappointed. This year's Bunker NYC party at Tangent Gallery quite literally rewired my brain and expanded my sonic palate in the best way. What was your biggest intention when recording this set, and what do you want listeners to feel or take away from it? 1morning: I just hope people find a track or two that in some way opens them up to something else. My favourite part of listening to a mix is the discovery aspect—the journey of discovering a new label or track is one of the most satisfying things about DJing. I enjoy the thrill of learning. Regal86: I love that the nature of the transitions are dry, raw, almost rough. No need to overthink or make it flashy. It’s not about impressing anyone, sólo sentir la música and let it flow. That's what makes it real.
translation.blurb: RA.999 launches with two of the 2020s' most exciting techno producers tearing a portal to the future.
